POL-562.title: IMPLEMENT: Foundation repair for Medic route health — unify RouteHealthState, retire dead Medic authority model, fix chart/escalation gaps, add route-exam mode
POL-562.body:
## Objective

Repair the inconsistencies and gaps in the current Medic / route-health / chart / run-health-symptom pipeline that were found during ANALYZE issue POL-553's audit, and add a canonical single-route "route exam" mode — establishing the foundation a future federated multi-Medic repository welfare check (deferred, see POL-553) would need to build on safely.

## Context

POL-553 asked to absorb DeepSec-style staged diagnostics into Medic and federate route health scans. Repo-analysis inspection (see `smartdocs/raw/analysis/pol-553-deepsec-medic-federation-analysis.md`) confirmed a real, working pipeline already exists end-to-end (run-health symptom → Medic chart → treatment packet → normal Foreman/Worker dispatch → QC), but it is 100% reactive to run-health failures (no proactive route exam), and several supporting pieces are broken or contradictory: two incompatible RouteHealthState enums; two contradictory Medic authority doctrines (an orphaned skill packet says Medic may self-implement, the live pipeline doctrine says it never does); a known open finalize-path escalation gap (POL-537); chart schema gaps (related_charts never parsed, chart-ID race condition); and an unbuilt graph subsystem with no build automation.

## Goal

One canonical RouteHealthState model, computed and persisted; one canonical, non-contradictory Medic authority doctrine; the finalize-path symptom-escalation gap closed; chart schema correctness gaps fixed; a working proactive single-route exam CLI entrypoint; and `polaris graph build` wired as a documented, repeatable prerequisite step.

## Scope

* src/cognition/route-cognition-delta.ts
* src/loop/worker.ts
* src/map/welfare.ts
* src/finalize/index.ts
* src/run-health/qc-escalation.ts
* src/medic/chart-schema.ts
* src/medic/chart-id.ts
* src/medic/treatment-packets.ts
* src/medic/run-health-consult.ts
* src/medic/POLARIS.md
* src/cli/medic.ts
* .polaris/roles/medic.md
* .polaris/skills/polaris-medic/**
* .polaris/skills/ROUTING.md
* src/types/result-packet.ts
* src/graph/**
* src/cli/graph.ts

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All 6 child issues are Done.
- [ ] RouteHealthState has one canonical definition, computed and persisted, and both welfare-check and the cognition path read the same source of truth.
- [ ] .polaris/roles/medic.md exists and matches the live, actually-wired doctrine; the orphaned polaris-medic skill packet and dead MedicPacket type are retired.
- [ ] The finalize-path run-health escalation gap (POL-537) is closed.
- [ ] Chart related_charts parsing and chart-ID concurrency are fixed.
- [ ] A working `polaris medic route-exam <route>` command produces a real chart with no triggering run-health symptom.
- [ ] .polaris/graph can be built via a documented, repeatable step, and getImpactedFiles() returns sane results against this repo.
- [ ] npm run build and npm test pass.

## Validation

* npm run build
* npm test

## Ordering

Cluster is internally ordered — children 1-4 are largely independent of each other; child 5 depends on children 1-2; child 6 is independent.

## Non-goals

* Do not implement federated/parallel multi-Medic scanning, cross-route referrals, deterministic aggregation, or a known-incident detector lifecycle — deferred to a follow-on ANALYZE pass.
* Do not produce a numeric token-savings benchmark in this cluster — no real route-exam packets exist yet to measure against.
* Do not fork or embed DeepSec code.

POL-563.title: Retire the orphaned Medic authority model and establish canonical Medic doctrine
POL-563.body:
## Objective

Remove the contradictory, orphaned Medic authority model and establish a single canonical Medic doctrine matching the live, actually-wired pipeline.

## Context

Part of IMPLEMENT parent POL-562 (from ANALYZE POL-553). `.polaris/skills/polaris-medic/` describes an older Medic model where Medic directly repairs implementation code, contradicting `src/medic/POLARIS.md`'s live doctrine (diagnose + hand off, never self-implement). `MedicPacket` (`src/types/result-packet.ts:110-125`) is referenced only in its own definition — no production code ever constructs or dispatches it. `.polaris/roles/medic.md`, referenced by the orphaned skill's own frontmatter, does not exist.

## Goal

One settled, non-contradictory Medic doctrine exists as .polaris/roles/medic.md, matching src/medic/POLARIS.md. The orphaned skill packet and dead type are removed.

## Scope

* .polaris/roles/medic.md
* .polaris/skills/polaris-medic/**
* .polaris/skills/ROUTING.md
* src/types/result-packet.ts

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] .polaris/roles/medic.md created, documenting the live doctrine, consistent with src/medic/POLARIS.md.
- [ ] .polaris/skills/polaris-medic/ directory removed (and any other doc referencing it as a live entrypoint corrected).
- [ ] MedicPacket interface and exclusively-related dead code in src/types/result-packet.ts removed.
- [ ] .polaris/skills/ROUTING.md's note about polaris-medic dispatch updated/removed.
- [ ] npm run build and npm test pass.

## Validation

* npm run build
* npm test
* grep -rn "MedicPacket" src (expect zero hits after removal)

## Ordering

None — independent; should land early since child POL-567 (route-exam CLI) should be built against the settled doctrine.

## Non-goals

* Do not change src/medic/POLARIS.md itself.
* Do not change the treatment-packet/dispatch pipeline.

POL-564.title: Unify RouteHealthState into one canonical schema and wire it end-to-end
POL-564.body:
## Objective

Replace the two incompatible RouteHealthState models with one canonical, computed, and persisted route health state.

## Context

Part of IMPLEMENT parent POL-562 (from ANALYZE POL-553). Two independent definitions exist: src/map/welfare.ts (3-state, used only by welfare-check) and src/cognition/route-cognition-delta.ts (5-state, fully built and unit-tested, but dead in production — applyRouteCognitionDelta() hardcodes healthState: undefined).

## Goal

The 5-state cognition model becomes canonical; applyRouteCognitionDelta() actually computes and persists it; welfare-check reads the same canonical state.

## Scope

* src/cognition/route-cognition-delta.ts
* src/cognition/route-cognition-delta.test.ts
* src/loop/worker.ts
* src/map/welfare.ts
* src/map/welfare.test.ts

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] src/map/welfare.ts's separate 3-state type removed; welfare-check uses the canonical 5-state type.
- [ ] applyRouteCognitionDelta() calls assessRouteHealth() and persists a real computed value.
- [ ] Computed route health state is persisted, readable by welfare-check and future callers.
- [ ] Tests cover the now-wired assessRouteHealth() call path.
- [ ] npm run build and npm test pass.

## Validation

* npm run build
* npm test
* npx vitest run src/cognition/route-cognition-delta.test.ts src/map/welfare.test.ts

## Ordering

None — independent; should land before any future federation/aggregation work (deferred).

## Non-goals

* Do not add new route health states.
* Do not implement cross-route aggregation or federation.

POL-565.title: Fix finalize-path run-health escalation gap (POL-537)
POL-565.body:
## Objective

Close the finalize-path gap where untrusted QC repair-loop outcomes hard-block with zero run-health symptom recorded and zero Medic escalation route.

## Context

Part of IMPLEMENT parent POL-562 (from ANALYZE POL-553), closing an already-diagnosed gap: smartdocs/raw/analysis/pol-537-finalize-medic-escalation-gap-analysis.md. runCompletedClusterQcWithRepair() never calls appendRepairLoopOutcomeSymptom() the way src/loop/parent.ts does for the loop run path.

## Goal

runCompletedClusterQcWithRepair() calls appendRepairLoopOutcomeSymptom() after runQcRepairLoop() returns, before validateQcRepairLoopGate() runs; also wire appendQcEscalationSymptoms() for the pre-repair result.

## Scope

* src/finalize/index.ts
* src/finalize/index.test.ts
* src/run-health/qc-escalation.ts

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] runCompletedClusterQcWithRepair() calls appendRepairLoopOutcomeSymptom() after runQcRepairLoop() returns, before the gate check.
- [ ] appendQcEscalationSymptoms() also called for the pre-repair runQcAtTrigger() result.
- [ ] Resulting symptom lands in run-health-report.json so a subsequent finalize retry's Medic gate can pick it up.
- [ ] New/updated test asserts the symptom is recorded on an untrusted outcome.
- [ ] npm run build and npm test pass.

## Validation

* npm run build
* npm test
* npx vitest run src/finalize/index.test.ts

## Ordering

None — independent.

## Non-goals

* Do not change qc-escalation.ts itself.
* Do not auto-dispatch Medic synchronously inside finalize.

POL-566.title: Fix chart schema correctness gaps: related_charts parsing and chart-ID concurrency
POL-566.body:
## Objective

Fix two chart-system correctness gaps that block any future concurrent/federated chart writer: related_charts front-matter is never actually parsed, and chart-ID generation has a race condition under concurrent callers.

## Context

Part of IMPLEMENT parent POL-562 (from ANALYZE POL-553). validateChart()'s hand-rolled parser always returns related_charts: [] regardless of content. generateNextChartId() rescans the charts directory per call with no lock.

## Goal

validateChart() correctly parses and validates related_charts against the 7 relationship types; chart-ID sequence claiming is race-safe under concurrent callers.

## Scope

* src/medic/chart-schema.ts
* src/medic/chart-schema.test.ts
* src/medic/chart-id.ts
* src/medic/chart-id.test.ts

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] validateChart() correctly parses related_charts front matter and validates entries against the 7 relationship types.
- [ ] generateNextChartId() (or replacement) is safe under concurrent callers, with a test simulating concurrent calls asserting no duplicate IDs.
- [ ] Existing charts still validate successfully after the parser fix.
- [ ] npm run build and npm test pass.

## Validation

* npm run build
* npm test
* npx vitest run src/medic/chart-schema.test.ts src/medic/chart-id.test.ts

## Ordering

None — independent.

## Non-goals

* Do not change the chart schema's required sections or front-matter fields.
* Do not implement actual concurrent/federated chart writing.

POL-567.title: Implement canonical single-route exam packet + polaris medic route-exam CLI command
POL-567.body:
## Objective

Add a proactive, single-route "route exam" mode so Medic can write a diagnostic chart for a route even with no triggering run-health symptom, reusing the existing chart/treatment-packet machinery unchanged.

## Context

Part of IMPLEMENT parent POL-562 (from ANALYZE POL-553). Chart creation today is 100% reactive to run-health symptoms. This child depends on POL-563 (settled Medic doctrine) and POL-564 (canonical RouteHealthState) landing first.

## Goal

A new `polaris medic route-exam <route>` command builds the minimal route-exam packet (POLARIS.md/SUMMARY.md, owned paths, chart history, relevant tests) and lets Medic write a chart proactively, reusing existing machinery unchanged. No graph dependency for this child.

## Scope

* src/cli/medic.ts
* src/medic/route-exam.ts
* src/medic/route-exam.test.ts
* src/medic/run-health-consult.ts

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `polaris medic route-exam <route>` command exists and builds a route-exam packet for the named route.
- [ ] Running it against a real route with no active run-health symptom produces a real chart via the existing chart machinery.
- [ ] The produced chart's health-state front matter uses the canonical RouteHealthState from POL-564.
- [ ] No changes to the treatment-packet/dispatch pipeline itself.
- [ ] npm run build and npm test pass.

## Validation

* npm run build
* npm test
* npx vitest run src/medic/route-exam.test.ts
* polaris medic route-exam src/medic (manual smoke check)

## Ordering

Depends on POL-563 and POL-564 landing first.

## Non-goals

* Do not implement impact-scan mode or repository-wide/federated scanning — deferred.
* Do not change how reactive chart creation works.

POL-568.title: Wire polaris graph build as a documented, repeatable prerequisite
POL-568.body:
## Objective

Make .polaris/graph a reliably buildable, documented prerequisite so future impact-scan / cross-route-referral work (deferred) has a real primitive to build on.

## Context

Part of IMPLEMENT parent POL-562 (from ANALYZE POL-553). src/graph/** is mature and polaris graph build is a real, non-stub command, but .polaris/graph has never been built in this repo, and no automation triggers it.

## Goal

`polaris graph build` is documented as an explicit, repeatable step and validated to actually work end-to-end against this repo.

## Scope

* src/cli/graph.ts
* src/graph/POLARIS.md
* docs (operator-facing doc for the graph build step)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Running `polaris graph build` against this repo succeeds and populates .polaris/graph.
- [ ] getImpactedFiles() (or equivalent) returns sane, non-empty results for at least one real changed-file scenario.
- [ ] A documented step explains when/how to run polaris graph build as a prerequisite for future impact-scan tooling.
- [ ] npm run build and npm test pass.

## Validation

* npm run build
* npm test
* polaris graph build (manual smoke check)

## Ordering

None — independent; can run in parallel.

## Non-goals

* Do not implement impact-scan mode or wire the graph into welfare-check/Medic.
* Do not add automatic/continuous graph rebuild.

POL-553.title: ANALYZE: Absorb DeepSec-style staged diagnostics into Medic and federate route health scans
